etymology.description: Depicts an inverted 子(子) (zǐ, "child") with lines representing fluid or hair -> newborn baby
etymology.interpretation: The character 㐬 is a pictogram representing a newborn baby, depicted in an upside-down position with the upper portion as an inverted 子(子) (zǐ, "child"), and the lower lines symbolizing amniotic fluid or hair. This imagery captures the typical birth position and context.
